2605.24678.title: Exploration of Perceptual Speech Features for Clinical Decision-Support in Mental Health Care
2605.24678.paper_summary: Speech and language technologies offer valuable opportunities for supporting mental health assessment through objective and interpretable cues. We present a systematic feature-based analysis framework leveraging perceptually grounded acoustic and linguistic characteristics, including prosody, vocal quality, semantic coherence, syntactic structure, and sarcasm. Using statistical analysis and interpretable machine learning (XGBoost with SHAP and LIME), we examine associations between speech features and validated symptom measures of depression, anxiety, and ADHD. Evaluated on both controlled benchmark datasets (StressID, DAIC-WOZ, Androids, EATD) and a real-world clinical dataset, the framework reveals stable and consistent relationships between symptom severity and vocal irregularities (e.g., shimmer, jitter), lexical-syntactic patterns, and affective tone. An ablation study conducted across all datasets further identifies the most informative feature groups. This work explores a transparent and clinically interpretable approach to speech-based mental health analysis.
2605.24678.paper_summary_zh: 語音和語言技術提供了通過客觀和可解釋的線索來支持心理健康評估的寶貴機會。<lb/>我們提出了一個基於特徵的系統分析框架，利用感知基礎的聲學和語言特徵，包括韻律、聲音質量、語義一致性、句法結構和諷刺。<lb/>通過統計分析和可解釋的機器學習（XGBoost 與 SHAP 和 LIME），我們檢查了語音特徵與經過驗證的抑鬱、焦慮和 ADHD 症狀測量之間的關聯。<lb/>在受控基準數據集（StressID、DAIC-WOZ、Androids、EATD）和一個現實世界臨床數據集上進行評估，該框架揭示了症狀嚴重性與聲音不規則性（例如，顫動、抖動）、詞彙句法模式和情感語調之間的穩定和一致的關係。<lb/>在所有數據集上進行的消融研究進一步確定了最具信息量的特徵組。<lb/>這項工作探索了一種透明且臨床可解釋的基於語音的心理健康分析方法。
2605.24678.author: Vassilis Lyberatos et.al.
2605.24678.authors: Vassilis Lyberatos, Edmund G. Dervakos, Eleni Adamidi, Athanasios Voulodimos, Giorgos Stamou
